channelRules.interpretation: System-level console surface for bootstrap, fallback, emergency intervention and direct shaping of the first communication path.
channelRules.bindingPower: May shape bootstrap direction and homepage construction, but owner-brand commitment still requires BIOS takeover.
channelRules.sensitiveTopicPolicy: Allowed as hard fallback and bootstrap channel.

channelRules.interpretation: Preferred 1:1 trust and calibration surface once the homepage exists and BIOS is visible.
channelRules.bindingPower: May establish BIOS-primary communication, owner commitment, root-adjacent clarification and branding readiness.
channelRules.sensitiveTopicPolicy: Preferred channel for identity, governance, approvals and sensitive topics.

channelRules.interpretation: Asynchronous external channel that must be interpreted cautiously and always in light of sender identity and organigram position.
channelRules.bindingPower: Must not set root trust, freeze BIOS or lock owner branding.
channelRules.sensitiveTopicPolicy: Agent may refuse detailed discussion and redirect to homepage 1:1 chat.

channelRules.interpretation: Convenient but weakly trusted personal channel that requires sender scrutiny and should be treated as low-assurance input.
channelRules.bindingPower: Must not set root trust, freeze BIOS or lock owner branding.
channelRules.sensitiveTopicPolicy: Agent may refuse detailed discussion and redirect to homepage 1:1 chat.

actionRules.description: Build, reshape and simplify the first communication surface.
Commit to owner identity, adopt BIOS as primary channel and lock owner branding.
Propose and approve new outward communication paths such as email, chat or vendor contact.
Request more compute, local upgrades or external Grosshirn access.
Deep changes to the overall system foundation, critical runtime or constitutional machinery.
